title: tools-panel
content:
Tools Panel

The Tools panel centralises measurement, dimensioning, and annotation workflows. Open it from the Tools button in the top toolbar, or click the current mode label in the status bar.

Tools

One-shot tools: click a tool to perform a single action, then the viewer returns to the default Navigate state (orbit and select). The active tool button is highlighted in green while you are placing points. Press Escape or tap Cancel to abort the current operation and return to Navigate (useful on tablets without a keyboard).

  • Measure – Click two points to place a distance measurement.

  • Angle – Click four points (two line segments) to measure the angle between them.

  • Dim (Flat) – Place a 2D CAD-style dimension (screen-aligned label).

  • Dim (3D) – Place a 3D-oriented CAD dimension in scene space.

  • Annotation (Flat) – Add a text note with a leader line (always faces the camera).

  • Annotation (3D) – Add a text note anchored in 3D space.

When no tool is active, you are in Navigate (the status bar shows this label). There is no separate Navigate button.

During Assembly Edit mode, tool buttons are disabled; the status bar shows Assembly Edit.

Options

  • Repeat tool – When enabled, after completing one measurement, dimension, or annotation the same tool stays active for the next one (useful for serial dimensioning).

  • Detect circle center – When enabled (visible while a Measure, Angle, or Dim tool is active), clicks snap to the centre of detected circular edges on the mesh surface.

  • Show behind model – Draw measurements and annotations on top of geometry even when they lie behind surfaces (disables depth test).

Visibility

  • Show measurements – Toggle all distance/angle measurements and CAD dimensions (flat and 3D).

  • Show annotations – Toggle all annotations (flat and 3D).

Actions

  • Clear all measurements… – Removes every distance measurement, angle measurement, and CAD dimension (2D and 3D) after confirmation.

  • Clear all annotations… – Removes every annotation (flat and 3D) after confirmation.

Dimension defaults

Default appearance for newly created dimensions. Use Apply to existing to restyle dimensions already in the scene.

  • Dimension (Flat) defaults – Size, text colour, background.

  • Dimension (3D) defaults – Size, rotation per orientation, orientation mode, colours.

  • Dimension markers defaults – Fixed/screen/world marker size and colour.

Annotation defaults

Default appearance for newly created annotations. Structure mirrors Dimension defaults.

  • Annotation (Flat) defaults – Size, text colour, background.

  • Annotation (3D) defaults – Size, rotation, orientation, colours.

  • Annotation markers defaults – Marker size and colour.
